reviewer: build-wave agent (real headless-Chrome first-visit + cached-reload verification, revision 1)

rubric.evidence: Real first-visit run in headless Chrome (WASM, no-GPU): clicked Download (~111 MB), reached loader state=ready, then the page auto-scored the default sentence 'The bank beat profit estimates and raised its full-year guidance.' → label POSITIVE, confidence 90.8%, backend WASM. Standalone transformers.js@3.7.5 check earlier returned negative 0.756 / neutral 0.144 / positive 0.100 for 'Shares plunged after the firm slashed its earnings forecast.' conformance (headless, no-GPU): 14✓ 0✗ 0▨ 9◍/23 — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in models/finbert-sentiment/worker.js via loadPipeline (text-classification, Xenova/finbert, q8).

rubric.evidence: Output is a 3-class softmax (positive/negative/neutral) summing to ~1; verified semantically correct in both directions — bullish sentence → POSITIVE 90.8%, bearish sentence → negative 0.756. top_k:3 confirmed to return all three classes (default returns only the winner).
rubric.notes: worker scoreMap indexes by label so class ordering never matters; renderClassBars shows the three real probabilities.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (Xenova/finbert) linked to card, declares-task (text-classification), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (new Worker) all pass — 5✓. At-a-glance table screenshot: reports/responsive/screens/finbert-sentiment/desktop.png.

Cached-reload test (same profile, Cache Storage persists): the page AUTO-initialised to state=ready with NO Download click; the only action button shown was 'Clear cached model', and the default sentence re-scored to POSITIVE 90.8% automatically. shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: Uses the shared lib/model-loader.js auto-init architecture; no hand-rolled Load button.

rubric.evidence: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live on #runStatus/#attrStatus and the loader status), errors-on-the-page=pass (scoring/attribution failures write to an .err status). Console clean on first visit AND reload (0 errors). runs-without-webgpu-fallback: this is a WASM-only model — it ran fully on the no-GPU runner, no WebGPU needed.
rubric.notes: Download progress surfaced via the shared loader <progress>; retry path provided by createModelLoader on failure.

rubric.evidence: Controls: textarea (labelled), 5 example chips (data-set), 'Explain the current text' occlusion button, plus the loader's Download/Clear buttons — all real, labelled, keyboard-operable. buttons-have-names=pass. Verified rendering desktop+mobile: reports/responsive/screens/finbert-sentiment/{desktop,mobile}.png.
rubric.notes: Occlusion attribution ('Explain') runs N+1 batched forward passes in the worker; exercised via the live status update.

rubric.evidence: Every visible score comes from the on-device pipeline; verified by watching the default and standalone runs produce genuinely different, semantically correct probabilities. No canned strings in the source.

images-have-alt=pass (no content images), buttons-have-names=pass. Meter labels sit OUTSIDE the coloured segments (tri-legend) to keep WCAG AA contrast; class names use the design-system good/warn/bad tokens. Live regions announce status. Light + dark both legible: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify-finbert-sentiment/{light,dark}.png.
rubric.notes: aria-hidden on the decorative tri-meter; the numeric class bars carry the accessible values.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check (automated): desktop (1280×800) clean, mobile (360×740 DPR3) clean — no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console+network clean. Screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/finbert-sentiment/{desktop,mobile}.png. support recorded needs-review pending a deeper agent tap-target pass.
rubric.notes: Class-bar grid uses fixed label columns + flexible track; chips wrap; logical properties throughout.

rubric.evidence: All inference (single classify, batch triage, and N+1 occlusion) runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only renders small DOM updates. Typing is debounced (350ms). No blocking loop on the main thread; INP stays low.
rubric.notes: guidance break-up-long-tasks applied via the worker.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 for long content; grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax()) for card rows; logical properties; avoid fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the comparison/class-bar layouts use flex-wrap and auto-fit grids with logical properties; meter is max-inline-size bounded. Verified no overflow at 360px and 1280px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep the main thread responsive; move heavy work off-thread and yield.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all FinBERT inference (including the N+1 occlusion batch) runs in a Web Worker; typing is debounced so scoring never blocks input.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; console clean during live scoring.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce errors and status changes in an accessible live region next to the action.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite on the run and attribution status lines and the loader status; failures add an .err class the user can read.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system, invariant 6).

openQuestions: Should the main page offer a compact inline FinBERT-vs-general contrast, or is keeping the head-to-head on the multi-model page the right scope?
Is the occlusion net-signal (logit(pos) − logit(neg)) the clearest attribution for a 3-class model, versus attributing each class separately?

followUpGoals.goal: Deeper mobile tap-target + focus-order audit on FinBERT (chips and loader buttons at 360px) to flip support from needs-review to ok with recorded evidence.
Add a FinBERT 'earnings-call paragraph' practical demo that segments a transcript into sentences and scores each, showing tone drift across the call.

summary: Revision-1 critique for finbert-sentiment (FinBERT, text-classification, WASM q8), backed by a real headless first-visit download → live output run (POSITIVE 90.8% on a bullish sentence; negative 0.756 on a bearish one), a cached-reload auto-init check (no Download button, 'Clear cached model' offered), conformance 14✓/0✗/9 manual, and a clean desktop+mobile responsive scan. Manual-evidenced dimensions were verified by the agent against real runs and screenshots. Strong, honest demo; mobile tap-target depth left as a follow-up.
